records.name: MITRE ATT&CK for blockchain threat contextualization
records.description: Maps generated blockchain and ERC threats to adversary tactics, techniques, and procedures when the threat involves attacker behavior beyond a pure smart-contract weakness.

mappings.mitre_context: Data manipulation or compromise of trusted external data sources.
mappings.use_in_etm: Attach as auxiliary context to the generated threat rationale and attack scenario.

mappings.mitre_context: Abuse of valid permissions, compromised accounts, or manipulation of decision-making workflows.
mappings.use_in_etm: Attach as auxiliary context when attack steps include off-chain compromise or social coordination.

mappings.mitre_context: Credential or key compromise enabling an attacker to act as a legitimate user.
mappings.use_in_etm: Attach as auxiliary context when the smart contract threat depends on account compromise.

usage_guidelines: Do not use MITRE ATT&CK as the primary source for ERC-specific vulnerabilities.
Use it to enrich attack scenarios and attack trees with real-world adversary behavior.
Prefer ERC, OWASP, SWC, CWE, and EthTrust for identifying smart-contract weaknesses.
